content.rendered:
In 1960, John D. Loudermilk wrote and recorded a semi-autobiographical blues song called “Tobacco Road.” It went nowhere.

Loudermilk’s song caught the attention of other musicians, though, and over the years it was covered by artists such as Jefferson Airplane, Eric Burdon & The Animals, Lou Rawls, and David Lee Roth, to name a few.

But it was The Nashville Teens who broke through with it in 1964, reaching #14 in the US and #6 in the UK.

On January 20, 1991, Bruce took a swing at it, too, joining Max Weinberg, Tim McLoone, and other local musicians at a benefit show at McLoone’s Rum Runner in Sea Bright.

Bruce would cover it once more a few years later, but this 1991 performance is the only one that was recorded. Take a listen below.

Tobacco Road
First performed: January 20, 1991 (Sea Bright, NJ)
Last performed: June 27, 1994 (West Hollywood, CA)

© November 21, 2018

excerpt.rendered: One time only (that was recorded, anyway): Bruce, Max, and Tim McLoone cover John D. Loudermilk’s “Tobacco Road.”

jetpack-related-posts.title: Long Tall Sally
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Lots of times with lots of people: "Long Tall Sally" is tried and true when Bruce joins fellow musicians for an impromptu jam. Check out some of his best performances inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Hippy Hippy Shake
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: From the Legends of Springsteen files: Bruce and Max join cover band Stand and Deliver at the Classics Cafe in Westwood for rocking rendition of "Hippy Hippy Shake."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Mystery Train
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: The biggest mystery about "Mystery Train" (besides why it's called that): why'd it take so long for Bruce to cover it?

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 8
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Bruce announces the reunion of the E Street Band! Plus: Watch Bruce improv a new "song" when nature calls during a show, catch two holiday shows, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 20
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Listen to a 1974 interview, a legendary '78 concert, and watch a 1994 holiday show appearance--all from this date.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me/RotD: Clarence Clemons and The Red Bank Rockers: Summer on Signal Hill
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: A lost original Springsteen song survives as a Red Bank Rockers instrumental--but whatever happened to the lyrics?
